facade_description: 311 Augusta Ave presents a house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 1 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a white-painted storefront with red lettering and graffiti-tagged side piers palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 0 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as an early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects architectural styles of the period. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructe
